description: Wiggum Engine v0.1 — verbose flag, engine rename, version tracking, SPRT comparison, Stockfish benchmarking, and version-report command

userStories.title: Rename MaterialEngine to Wiggum Engine
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the engine consistently named 'Wiggum Engine' so that match logs and UCI GUIs display the correct identity.
acceptanceCriteria: Struct MaterialEngine in chess-engine/src/engine.rs renamed to WiggumEngine
name() returns 'Wiggum Engine v0.1'
UCI binary (chess-engine/src/main.rs) responds to 'uci' with 'id name Wiggum Engine v0.1' and 'id author chess-ic'
CLI --about text updated from 'MaterialEngine' to 'Wiggum Engine'
All tests referencing MaterialEngine or material_engine updated to WiggumEngine
cargo test --workspace passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD US-011. Pure rename — no logic changes.

userStories.title: Add --verbose flag to chess-runner match command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to control chess-runner output verbosity so that I can run quiet batch matches or verbose single-game debugging.
acceptanceCriteria: New --verbose flag added to MatchArgs in chess-runner/src/main.rs
Without --verbose: each game prints exactly one line 'Game N/Total: <result> | Score: W-L-D' and a final summary block; board and move lines are suppressed
With --verbose: existing behavior preserved — board diagram and move line printed on every move, game result printed at end of each game
PrintObserver is replaced or refactored to support both modes (e.g. two structs or a verbose: bool field)
The current always-verbose PrintObserver is removed; verbose is opt-in
Running chess-runner match --engine1 X --engine2 Y --games 3 without --verbose produces exactly 3 result lines plus 1 summary block
cargo test --workspace passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD US-010. No dependency on US-001.

userStories.title: Create chess-engine/versions/ folder structure
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a dedicated versioned folder structure so I can track what changed and how each version performed over time.
acceptanceCriteria: Directory chess-engine/versions/v0.1/ exists
File chess-engine/versions/v0.1/CHANGES.md exists and documents: initial implementation, material evaluation + negamax depth-4, no alpha-beta
File chess-engine/versions/README.md exists and explains the folder convention: one subfolder per version, each with CHANGES.md and report.md
A .gitkeep placeholder exists in chess-engine/versions/v0.1/ if no report.md exists yet
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD US-012. Pure file/folder creation — no code changes.

userStories.title: Add set_option method to UciEngine in chesslib
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to send arbitrary UCI setoption commands to a subprocess engine so I can configure Stockfish skill levels from the match runner.
acceptanceCriteria: UciEngine in chesslib/src/uci_engine.rs gains a public async method: pub async fn set_option(&mut self, name: &str, value: &str) -> Result<()>
Method sends 'setoption name <name> value <value>\n' to engine stdin and flushes
No response is expected (UCI spec: setoption has no reply); method returns after flush
The Engine trait in chesslib/src/engine.rs is NOT modified — set_option is a concrete method on UciEngine only
Mock engine in uci_engine.rs tests already silently ignores unknown commands (verify this is the case)
Unit test added: sends setoption name Skill Level value 10 to mock engine without error
cargo test --workspace passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD US-017. Prerequisite for US-007 (benchmark script). The mock engine uses a wildcard case that ignores unknown commands — confirm before writing the test.

userStories.title: Implement SPRT subcommand in chess-runner
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a SPRT-based match mode so I can efficiently determine whether a new engine version is an improvement without running an arbitrary fixed number of games.
acceptanceCriteria: New subcommand 'chess-runner sprt' added with args: --engine1, --engine2, --time (default 10000), --inc (default 100), --elo0 (default 0), --elo1 (default 5), --alpha (default 0.05), --beta (default 0.05), --timeout (default 5000), optional --output (CSV path)

LLR is computed after each completed game using the trinomial (W/D/L) model: score = (wins + 0.5*draws) / total; elo_diff = -400 * log10(1/score - 1); LLR approximation tracks wins/draws/losses separately
Full LLR formula: LLR = W*ln(p1_w/p0_w) + D*ln(p1_d/p0_d) + L*ln(p1_l/p0_l) where p0/p1 are expected W/D/L probabilities under H0 (elo0) and H1 (elo1) using the logistic Elo model
LLR bounds: lower = ln(beta / (1 - alpha)), upper = ln((1 - beta) / alpha)
After each game, prints one line: '[Game N] LLR: X.XX / [lower, upper] | W:x D:x L:x'
Match stops automatically when LLR crosses either bound
Final output states: 'SPRT Result: H1 accepted (improvement confirmed)' or 'SPRT Result: H0 accepted (no improvement detected)'
If --output provided, appends one row to a sprt_results.csv with columns: timestamp, engine1_name, engine2_name, games_played, wins, draws, losses, elo0, elo1, sprt_result
Creates sprt_results.csv with header if it does not exist
Unit test: LLR boundary values for alpha=0.05 beta=0.05 are approximately [-2.944, 2.944]
Unit test: LLR computation with known W/D/L counts returns expected value
cargo test --workspace passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD US-013. Colors alternate each game pair (same as match subcommand). Handle the edge case where score=1.0 or score=0.0 to avoid log(0) — clamp to a small epsilon.

userStories.title: Add version-report subcommand to chess-runner
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a version-report command that generates a rich markdown report for a specific engine version so I have a self-contained snapshot of its performance.
acceptanceCriteria: New subcommand 'chess-runner version-report' with args: --version (e.g. 'v0.1'), --engine-name (optional, e.g. 'Wiggum Engine v0.1'), --matches-csv (path to regular match CSV), --sprt-csv (optional path to sprt_results.csv), --output (markdown output path)
Reads matches CSV and filters rows where engine1_name or engine2_name contains --engine-name (or --version if --engine-name not provided)

Generated markdown includes: H1 header with version name and generation date; summary table with columns: Opponent, Games, Wins, Draws, Losses, Win%; overall win rate across all opponents; best matchup (highest win%); worst matchup (lowest win%)
If --sprt-csv is provided, adds a SPRT Results section: table with columns: Opponent, Games, W, D, L, Result
Ends with an empty '## Notes' section for manual annotation
If --output path does not exist, creates it (including parent dirs)
Handles missing or empty CSVs gracefully with a message in the markdown
cargo test --workspace passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD US-015. Depends on US-005 for SPRT CSV format. The --engine-name matching should be case-insensitive substring match to handle name variations.

userStories.title: Benchmark shell script for new engine versions
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a shell script that automates benchmarking a new engine version against Stockfish at multiple skill levels so I get consistent, reproducible results.
acceptanceCriteria: Script at scripts/benchmark-version.sh is created and executable (chmod +x)

Script accepts flags: --version (e.g. v0.1), --engine (path to engine binary), --prev-engine (optional, path to previous version binary), --stockfish (path to stockfish binary), --games (default 100), --output-dir (default chess-engine/versions/<version>)
If --prev-engine is provided, runs 'chess-runner sprt --engine1 <engine> --engine2 <prev-engine> --time 10000 --inc 100' and saves output
Runs 'chess-runner match' (not SPRT) for each of four Stockfish difficulty levels: 1500, 2000, 2500, max

For rated levels (1500/2000/2500): sets UCI_LimitStrength=true and UCI_Elo=<N> on Stockfish using setoption before the match — note: chess-runner does not yet support passing setoption args; the script must use a wrapper or document this as a known limitation if not supported
For max: sets UCI_LimitStrength=false
Uses time control 10s + 0.1s inc for all matches
Appends all match results to --output-dir/results.csv using chess-runner --output flag
Prints a summary to stdout when all matches complete
Script has a usage comment block at the top explaining all flags
Typecheck passes (N/A for shell — verify script has no syntax errors with bash -n scripts/benchmark-version.sh)
userStories.notes: PRD US-014. Depends on US-004 (set_option) and US-005 (sprt subcommand). IMPORTANT: chess-runner match does not support passing setoption to engines. The benchmark script should use a thin wrapper script per Stockfish level (e.g. a wrapper that sends setoption before UCI handshake) OR document this as a limitation. The simplest approach: create wrapper shell scripts like scripts/stockfish-1500.sh that exec stockfish but intercept uci/setoption.

userStories.title: Generate and commit v0.1 benchmark report
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to run the benchmark for v0.1 and commit the report so we have a baseline for measuring future improvements.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/benchmark-version.sh runs successfully for v0.1 with the Stockfish binary at engines/stockfish
chess-runner version-report generates chess-engine/versions/v0.1/report.md
report.md contains: the version header, a summary table with results vs Stockfish at all four levels, overall win rate
chess-engine/versions/v0.1/report.md is committed to the repository
Any .gitkeep placeholder in chess-engine/versions/v0.1/ is removed once report.md exists
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD US-016. Depends on all prior stories. Requires engines/stockfish to be present. This story may require manual steps (running the script) — Ralph should run it and commit the output.
